<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Let me look at the ASCII around the character and green frame
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCurrent board (rows 29-49, cols 35-58):")
for i in range(29, 50):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {lines[i][35:59]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try rotating the character to face down
# Currently facing right, need to rotate clockwise to face down
# SPACE rotates the character

action(['SPACE'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After SPACE:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
print(f"  Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Let me look at the character's shape
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCharacter shape (rows 32-46, cols 29-43):")
for i in range(32, 47):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {lines[i][29:44]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try rotating the character to face down
# Currently facing right, need to rotate clockwise to face down
# SPACE rotates the character

action(['SPACE'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After SPACE:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
print(f"  Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Let me look at the character's shape
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCharacter shape (rows 32-46, cols 29-43):")
for i in range(32, 47):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {lines[i][29:44]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
